name: Sang Hyang Widhi
description: Sang Hyang Widhi Wasa is the supreme and absolute God in Balinese Hinduism. Considered the source of all existence, He is formless, beyond comprehension, and transcends all deities and concepts. He is not anthropomorphic, existing as the ultimate reality and the underlying principle of the universe. Sang Hyang Widhi is the creator, preserver, and destroyer of all things, embodying the Trimurti (Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva) as one indivisible entity. While He is formless, He is often represented symbolically through sacred objects and rituals. Prayers are offered to Him through various forms and manifestations of other deities, who are seen as aspects or emanations of His divine power. His worship emphasizes the importance of dharma (righteous conduct), karma (action and consequence), and reincarnation. Temples (Pura) are dedicated to other deities, but ultimately, all prayers are directed to Sang Hyang Widhi, the ultimate source of all blessings and the foundation of Balinese spiritual life. He is the unmanifest Brahman, the eternal and unchanging truth behind the ever-changing world. He is also known as Acintya, meaning 'inconceivable' or 'unthinkable,' reflecting His transcendent nature. He is invoked in all ceremonies and rituals, acknowledging His omnipresence and supreme authority.
subtitle: The Supreme God of Balinese Hinduism

keyMyths.title: The Unmanifest Origin
keyMyths.description: Sang Hyang Widhi is believed to be self-existent and the source of all creation. He is not born, nor does he die, existing eternally as the ultimate reality.
keyMyths.source: Balinese Hindu Texts and Oral Traditions

keyMyths.title: Manifestation through Deities
keyMyths.description: While formless, Sang Hyang Widhi manifests through various deities who represent different aspects of His power. These deities are worshipped as intermediaries to the Supreme God.

keyMyths.title: The Cycle of Creation and Destruction
keyMyths.description: Sang Hyang Widhi embodies the Trimurti, representing the cyclical process of creation, preservation, and destruction. This cycle ensures the continuous renewal and balance of the universe.
